tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, ketonuria, and glucosuria, with low urine specific gravity and low HbA1c. These findings suggest possible acute or chronic renal impairment and impaired glucose handling, but without evidence of infection. The brown, cloudy urine with ammoniacal odor may indicate underlying renal or hepatic dysfunction. Further clinical correlation and assessment of renal and hepatic function are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
